title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
At Scotland Yard, the narrator sits with Jenny Hobbs while Stanley Bill interrogates Samuel Kid. Poirot reveals that Jenny Hobbs and Nancy Duane conspired, with the assistance of Samuel Kid, to kill Harriet Westphal, Ada Gransbury, and Richard Nix. During this conspiracy, Jenny pretended to be an accomplice to Richard Nix in order to manipulate him.

Poirot clarifies that Jenny’s motive was not jealousy, as she had previously claimed, but a selfless desire to protect the reputation of Patrick Ives. To prevent gossip regarding Nancy Duane’s late-night visits to the vicarage, Jenny fabricated a rumor that Patrick was charging people to communicate with the deceased. She specifically targeted Harriet Westphal, who was obsessed with her late husband, George. Jenny suggested to Harriet that Patrick could facilitate communication with George for a fee, knowing Harriet would pay any amount to achieve this.

Jenny admits she made this promise to Harriet without Patrick's consent to keep Harriet silent and prevent the scandal from spreading. Although Patrick found the idea of pretending to channel spirits to be immoral and terrifying, he instructed Jenny to maintain the lie to avoid suspicion. He insisted that she tell Harriet his services were unavailable rather than admitting the communication was a fabrication.
